title: Production Identity & Access Hardening Pack
decision_rationale: GL-219 adds narrow identity/access posture helpers, fail-closed production-like startup checks for unsupported external identity settings, a local-only dry-run gate, documentation, and tests. It does not add a real identity provider or claim production readiness.

current_admin_operator_auth_model.admin_auth: GRANTLAYER_ADMIN_TOKEN Bearer token with constant-time comparison and fail-closed admin route guards.
current_admin_operator_auth_model.operator_auth: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 hashed bearer tokens with lookup hash narrowing, active-row filtering, expiry handling, and tenant binding.
current_admin_operator_auth_model.tenant_context: Server-derived from operator row or demo admin-token context; request headers cannot override it.

identity_access_gap_assessment.real_data_boundary: Real customer/private grant/institutional data remains no-go.

implemented_hardening_summary: Added backend/src/identity_access.py with safe posture helpers and JWT validation requirements.
Added config.startup_errors() fail-closed checks for unsupported OAuth/OIDC/JWT enablement and provider config in production-like modes.
Added scripts/ops/gl219_identity_access_gate.py as a local-only dry-run/plan identity/access gate.
Added GL-219 documentation, JSON artifact, and tests.
oauth_oidc_jwt_validation_posture: explicit issuer allowlist with exact issuer match
explicit audience allowlist with exact audience match
expiration, not-before, and issued-at validation with bounded clock skew
algorithm allowlist that rejects none and unexpected algorithms
JWKS or key material selection by key id with unknown-key denial
key rotation and retired-key handling with a documented overlap window
tenant/workspace mapping from trusted claims only
revocation/deprovisioning lifecycle for operators and admin access
safe logging that omits raw tokens, token hashes, auth headers, and keys
provider outage and metadata-fetch failures deny protected access

issuer_audience_expiry_key_rotation_expe.issuer: exact-match allowlist required before token acceptance
issuer_audience_expiry_key_rotation_expe.audience: exact-match allowlist required before token acceptance
issuer_audience_expiry_key_rotation_expe.expiry: exp, nbf, iat, and bounded clock skew required before claim use
issuer_audience_expiry_key_rotation_expe.key_rotation: kid selection, unknown-key denial, retired-key denial, overlap window, cache invalidation, and emergency retirement required
issuer_audience_expiry_key_rotation_expe.current_state: requirements only; no live provider config

production_readiness_impact.summary: Identity/access blocker is materially reduced by explicit posture and fail-closed misconfiguration checks, but production IAM remains incomplete.

controlled_preview_impact.summary: Improves local misconfiguration safety and reviewer clarity.

remaining_identity_access_blockers: Production OAuth/OIDC/JWT validator is not implemented.
Static admin token remains a production blocker.
SSO, MFA, named admin identities, and account lifecycle are absent.
Dual-control break-glass, access review, and emergency governance are absent.
Automated operator deprovisioning and rotation workflows are absent.
Workspace enforcement, database row-level security, and tenant lifecycle remain blockers.
Production monitoring, alerting, SIEM/SOC, incident rota, and access anomaly response remain incomplete.
Real customer/private grant/institutional data remains blocked.

risk_register.risk: External identity tokens could be misconfigured before validator exists.
JWT issuer/audience/expiry/key rotation requirements are not implemented.
Workspace and tenant lifecycle remain incomplete.

findings: Current admin/operator model remains a controlled-preview bearer-token baseline.
Production-like runtime now fails closed if unsupported external identity settings are configured.
Local GL-219 gate reports required identity artifacts and remaining blockers without network calls or raw token handling.
recommended_next_issues: Dedicated production identity-provider design gate for OAuth/OIDC/JWT validation without live vendor configuration.
Operator/admin lifecycle and rotation workflow hardening.
Workspace enforcement, tenant lifecycle, and RLS hardening.
Production access governance, break-glass, alerting, and incident ownership gate.
